_disclaimer: SPECULATIVE forward conviction-research over a live-discovered small/mid-cap universe. NOT a backtested edge; small-caps carry liquidity, fraud and total-loss risk. Research leads, you decide. Manual trades only.

discoveries.name: CorMedix Inc.
discoveries.valuation: fwd EV/EBITDA ~7.6x (TTM, just turned profitable); price target $14 (~+61%)
discoveries.thesis: Single-product biopharma at the moment its product catches fire: DefenCath (catheter-related bloodstream-infection prevention) is in early commercial ramp and Q1'26 revenue blew past consensus by ~21% ($127M vs $105M est) while the company flipped to GAAP profit. The stock is down ~47% over the past year, so the fundamental inflection is happening BEFORE the price — the classic cheap-and-quietly-improving setup, with all 8 covering analysts on Buy and a $14 target.
discoveries.key_risk: Essentially a one-product story; any reimbursement, competitive, manufacturing or label setback for DefenCath hits the whole thesis. Revenue can be lumpy quarter to quarter.
early_signals: Q1'26 revenue +21% vs estimate, EPS beat +23%
Crossed from losses into GAAP profitability
Stock -47% over 1y while fundamentals inflect (price lagging the turn)
8/8 analysts Buy, consensus target ~+61%

news.headline: As US profit margins near record highs, these small-cap stocks lead on profitability (CorMedix among A+ profitability names)

discoveries.name: PowerFleet, Inc.
discoveries.valuation: EV/S ~5.9x, EV/EBITDA ~19x; loss narrowing to profit by FY27; target $7 (~+76%)
discoveries.thesis: Wireless-IoT/telematics roll-up (PowerFleet + Pointer + MiX) whose mix is shifting to high-margin recurring software — service revenue is now ~81% of the total and grew 14% YoY with the Q4 exit-rate accelerating. The stock is still down on the year and GAAP-lossmaking on acquisition intangibles, but estimates show it crossing into profit through FY27. A beaten-down, under-covered (5 analysts) consolidator that should re-rate as the recurring base compounds; all 5 analysts Buy with a $7 target.
discoveries.key_risk: Still GAAP-unprofitable with heavy intangibles and integration risk from the MiX/Pointer mergers; micro-cap liquidity and a stretched EV/sales if the profit cross-over slips.
early_signals: Service (recurring) revenue 81% of mix, +14% YoY
Q4 total revenue +11% YoY, growth accelerating into the exit rate
Signed multi-year deals with two of the world's largest brands
5/5 analysts Buy, target ~+76%

news.headline: PowerFleet Q4 2025 earnings call: service revenue now 81% of mix, growth accelerating into FY27

discoveries.name: IDT Corporation
discoveries.valuation: fwd P/E ~14x; net cash $246M and no debt; legacy telecom masks the growth segments
discoveries.thesis: A genuinely under-followed (just 2 analysts) telecom/fintech where a melting legacy long-distance business hides three high-margin compounders — NRS point-of-sale, BOSS Money fintech and net2phone UCaaS — that are growing fast. Q3'26 beat on both lines, EBITDA and operating cash flow hit records, and the balance sheet is fortress-like ($246M cash, no debt). At ~14x forward earnings with the growth segments approaching the size of the shrinking core, it's a cheap sum-of-the-parts that the market hasn't repriced.
discoveries.key_risk: Legacy Traditional Communications revenue is in structural decline; if it rolls over faster than the growth segments scale, consolidated numbers stall. Family-controlled, very thinly covered.
early_signals: Only 2 analysts cover it — true under-the-radar
Record adjusted EBITDA and operating cash flow on the trailing year
Q3'26 EPS +5.6% vs estimate, revenue beat ~+2.5%
$246M cash / zero debt vs $1.4B market cap

news.headline: IDT investor conference: $1.2B-cap with strong balance sheet and increasing profitability; high-margin segments growing rapidly

discoveries.name: Collegium Pharmaceutical, Inc.
discoveries.valuation: fwd P/E ~4-5x; EV/EBITDA ~17x TTM and falling; ~45% EBITDA margins; target $58 (~+73%)
discoveries.thesis: Deeply de-rated specialty-pharma cash machine: Q1'26 EPS beat by ~16% and revenue by ~5%, EBITDA margins sit near 45%, and forward EPS estimates imply a single-digit P/E. The stock trades near 52-week lows (~20% of its range) despite rising estimates and aggressive buybacks — a cheap, cash-generative name where sentiment is washed out and the consensus target sits ~70% higher.
discoveries.key_risk: Opioid-franchise (Xtampza/Nucynta) headline and legal/ESG overhang plus a levered balance sheet from acquisitions; pipeline/diversification must deliver before the core matures.
early_signals: Q1'26 EPS beat +16%, revenue beat +5%, estimates rising
Forward P/E in the mid-single digits with ~45% EBITDA margins
Trades near 52-week lows while fundamentals improve
Strong free-cash-flow yield funding buybacks

news.headline: Small-cap stocks with A+ profitability grade — Collegium Pharmaceutical named among them

discoveries.name: Electromed, Inc.
discoveries.valuation: P/E ~30x but debt-free with net cash; consistent double-digit beats
discoveries.thesis: A tiny, debt-free niche medical-device compounder (SmartVest airway-clearance therapy) that quietly crushes estimates quarter after quarter — Q3'26 EPS beat by ~35% on a ~5.5% revenue beat — yet only 5 analysts follow it. Net-cash balance sheet, expanding margins and a direct-to-patient model in a sticky reimbursed market; the kind of under-owned profitable micro-cap that compounds long before it shows up on screens.
discoveries.key_risk: Trades near 52-week highs after an ~82% run, so entry timing matters; concentrated single-product, reimbursement-dependent micro-cap with thin liquidity.
early_signals: Q3'26 EPS beat +35%, revenue beat +5.5%
Debt-free, net-cash balance sheet; consistently profitable
Only 5 analysts — under-covered compounder
All 5 analysts Buy

news.headline: Electromed investor conference: 'pretty unique for a small-cap company to be so profitable'

discoveries.name: Graham Corporation
discoveries.thesis: Defense/space turbomachinery supplier (Navy propulsion, cryogenics, space) with a record $533M backlog compounding ~20% a year and ~85% tied to defense — exceptional multi-year revenue visibility, and Q4 beat on both lines. The least 'early' name here: it has already run ~128% in a year and trades at a rich ~60x forward P/E near 52-week highs, so it's included for the genuine backlog/earnings-ramp story rather than valuation.
discoveries.key_risk: Valuation is full and the stock is extended near highs; margins are still thin, so any execution slip on the backlog ramp could de-rate it sharply. Watch for a pullback rather than chasing.
early_signals: Record $533M backlog growing ~20% CAGR, book-to-bill ~1.3x
85% of backlog is defense (multi-year visibility into FY28)
Q4 EPS beat +10%, revenue beat +12%
Forward EPS ramping FY26 -> FY28

news.headline: Graham Corporation 2026 Investor Day: $533M backlog, ~20% CAGR, book-to-bill 1.3x, 85% defense
